settings.title: Book a Spring Wills Review With Us
settings.excerpt: Through April we are offering fixed-fee will reviews for anyone whose circumstances have changed.
settings.body:
A will written ten years ago may no longer say what you want it to. Marriages, children, house moves, and new businesses all change the picture, and many people never go back to update the document.

For the whole of April, Greystone is running fixed-fee will reviews. We will check that your existing will still reflects your wishes and flag anything that needs attention, with no obligation to make changes.

When a will tends to fall out of date

Life rarely stands still long enough for a single document to keep up. The events that most often leave a will saying the wrong thing are also the most common ones.

  • Marriage, divorce, or a new partner
  • The arrival of children or grandchildren
  • Buying, selling, or inheriting property
  • Starting or selling a business

Any one of these can change who should inherit, who should be in charge, and how things are divided. A will that ignores them can create exactly the confusion it was meant to prevent.

What the appointment involves

It is a straightforward appointment, usually under an hour. We read through what you have, ask a few questions about what has changed since it was written, and tell you plainly whether it still does its job.

If everything is in order, you leave reassured and nothing more is owed. If something needs updating, you will know exactly what and why. If you are not sure whether yours is up to date, that uncertainty is reason enough to come in.
